entries.title: Favicon hash calculator (Shodan-compatible)
entries.body:
Computes the **favicon hash** the same way Shodan does: it fetches the target's `/favicon.ico`, encodes the body to base64 **with line breaks** (this matters: `base64.encodebytes`, not `b64encode`!), takes `mmh3.hash` and casts it to a **signed** 32-bit int. Then it prints a ready-made dork `http.favicon.hash:<int>` for Shodan/ZoomEye/FOFA. The idea: a WAF/CDN hides the origin IP, but the origin server's favicon is often the same as the frontend's. By finding hosts with the same hash on Shodan, you find an IP that responds bypassing the CDN.

> The `http.favicon.hash:` facet search on Shodan itself is **paid** (it needs query credits, which a free account does not give). The script only COMPUTES the hash offline; you run the Shodan/FOFA query yourself via the UI/paid API. ZoomEye/FOFA accept the same hash.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# favicon_hash.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Compute Shodan-compatible favicon hash (mmh3 over base64-with-newlines, signed int32).
import argparse, base64, struct, sys, urllib.request, urllib.parse, ssl
try:
    import mmh3
except ImportError:
    sys.exit("[!] need: pip install mmh3")

def fetch(url, timeout):
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    ctx.check_hostname = False
    ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "ARS3NAL-favicon/1.0"})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout, context=ctx) as r:
        return r.read()

def fav_hash(raw):
    # Shodan encodes the icon with base64.encodebytes (keeps the 76-col newlines + trailing \n)
    b64 = base64.encodebytes(raw)
    u = mmh3.hash(b64)            # mmh3.hash already returns a signed int32 on most builds
    # normalize to signed int32 explicitly so the result matches Shodan regardless of build
    s = struct.unpack(">i", struct.pack(">I", u & 0xFFFFFFFF))[0]
    return s

def target_to_favurl(t):
    if t.startswith("http://") or t.startswith("https://"):
        p = urllib.parse.urlparse(t)
        if p.path in ("", "/"):
            return f"{p.scheme}://{p.netloc}/favicon.ico"
        return t
    return f"https://{t}/favicon.ico"

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL Shodan favicon-hash calculator")
    ap.add_argument("target", help="host, URL, or path to a local .ico file")
    ap.add_argument("-f", "--file", action="store_true", help="treat target as a local icon file")
    ap.add_argument("-t", "--timeout", type=int, default=15)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    try:
        raw = open(a.target, "rb").read() if a.file else fetch(target_to_favurl(a.target), a.timeout)
    except Exception as e:
        sys.exit(f"[!] fetch failed: {e}")
    h = fav_hash(raw)
    print(f"[*] bytes={len(raw)}  favicon.hash={h}")
    print(f"[=] shodan : http.favicon.hash:{h}")
    print(f"[=] fofa   : icon_hash=\"{h}\"")
    print(f"[=] zoomeye: iconhash=\"{h}\"")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
pip install mmh3
python3 favicon_hash.py https://target.example.com
python3 favicon_hash.py -f /path/to/favicon.ico
# then paste the printed dork into Shodan/FOFA (the Shodan facet is paid)
```

entries.title: Origin-IP recon behind CDN (crt.sh + DNS history + SPF/MX leak)
entries.deps: sudo apt install -y dnsutils whois curl
entries.body:
Harvests **origin-IP candidates** for a domain behind a CDN from several free sources and resolves historical/leaked names, flagging IPs that **do not belong to the CDN**. The logic: (1) `crt.sh` certificates yield related hosts that often point straight at the origin; (2) mail records (`MX`, `SPF` in TXT) leak real IPs, since mail usually goes around the CDN; (3) typical subdomains `direct.`/`origin.`/`mail.`/`cpanel.` frequently have an A record pointing at the origin. Every IP found is resolved and checked against a list of CDN ranges: everything NOT in a CDN is printed as `[!] CANDIDATE ORIGIN`.

> This is a heuristic, not a guarantee. Always verify a candidate with the `verify_origin.sh` script (Host header via `--resolve`). The CDN ranges here are determined from the ASN description via `whois`; if there is no network, replace the check with your own static list of prefixes.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# origin_recon.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Harvest origin-IP candidates behind a CDN: crt.sh + DNS history names + SPF/MX leaks.
set -u
DOMAIN="${1:?usage: origin_recon.sh <domain>}"
CDN_RE='cloudflare|cloudfront|akamai|fastly|incapsula|imperva|sucuri|stackpath|edgecast|google|azure|amazon'

note(){ printf '%s\n' "[*] $*" >&2; }
resolve(){ dig +short "$1" A 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$'; }

# is this IP part of a known CDN? (whois org/netname match) -> returns 0 if CDN
is_cdn(){
  whois "$1" 2>/dev/null | grep -iqE "$CDN_RE"
}

names_file="$(mktemp)"; ips_file="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$names_file" "$ips_file"' EXIT

note "crt.sh certificate names for $DOMAIN"
curl -s --max-time 30 "https://crt.sh/?q=%25.${DOMAIN}&output=json" \
  | tr ',' '\n' | grep -oiE "[a-z0-9._-]+\.${DOMAIN}" | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' | sort -u >> "$names_file"

note "common direct/origin subdomain guesses"
for p in direct origin origin-www mail smtp mx cpanel webmail ftp dev staging server host vpn; do
  echo "${p}.${DOMAIN}" >> "$names_file"
done
echo "$DOMAIN" >> "$names_file"

note "SPF / MX leak (mail usually bypasses the CDN)"
dig +short "$DOMAIN" MX 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/\.$//' >> "$names_file"
dig +short "$DOMAIN" TXT 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' '\n' \
  | grep -oiE 'ip4:[0-9.]+' | cut -d: -f2 >> "$ips_file"

note "resolving names"
sort -u "$names_file" | while read -r n; do
  [ -z "$n" ] && continue
  resolve "$n" | while read -r ip; do echo "$ip" >> "$ips_file"; done
done

echo
echo "[=] origin candidates (non-CDN IPs):"
sort -u "$ips_file" | while read -r ip; do
  [ -z "$ip" ] && continue
  if is_cdn "$ip"; then
    echo "    [cdn]  $ip"
  else
    echo "    [!] CANDIDATE ORIGIN  $ip"
  fi
done
```

Run:
```bash
# needs dig (dnsutils), whois, curl
sudo apt install -y dnsutils whois curl
./origin_recon.sh target.example.com
# run each candidate through verify_origin.sh
```

entries.title: Verify origin IP via curl --resolve diff
entries.body:
Confirms that a found IP really is the **origin** behind the CDN and not a random server. It makes the same HTTPS request twice: once through the CDN (as usual), and a second time forced onto the candidate IP via `curl --resolve HOST:443:IP`, supplying the real Host header. Then it compares: HTTP status, body length, the `Server` header, the presence of CDN headers (`cf-ray`, `x-amz-cf-id`, etc.) and the body hash. If the candidate returns the same content but **without** CDN headers, it is an origin you can hit directly (handy for bypassing a WAF at the CDN level).

> Many origin servers answer `403/421` on a wrong Host or on a direct IP altogether. If the body hash matches with the correct Host, that is a strong signal. Compare against the exact same path (`-p /`), not the IP's default page.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify_origin.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Confirm a candidate origin IP behind a CDN via curl --resolve diff.
set -u
usage(){ echo "usage: verify_origin.sh <hostname> <candidate-ip> [path] [port]"; exit 1; }
HOST="${1:-}"; IP="${2:-}"; PATHQ="${3:-/}"; PORT="${4:-443}"
[ -z "$HOST" ] || [ -z "$IP" ] && usage
SCHEME="https"; [ "$PORT" = "80" ] && SCHEME="http"

probe(){ # $1 = curl extra args; prints "status|len|server|cdnhdr|bodyhash"
  local extra="$1" hdr body
  hdr="$(curl -s -k -D - -o /tmp/.vo_body $extra --max-time 20 \
        -H "User-Agent: ARS3NAL-verify/1.0" "${SCHEME}://${HOST}${PATHQ}" 2>/dev/null)"
  local status len server cdn bh
  status="$(printf '%s' "$hdr" | awk 'NR==1{print $2}')"
  len="$(wc -c < /tmp/.vo_body | tr -d ' ')"
  server="$(printf '%s' "$hdr" | grep -i '^server:' | head -1 | cut -d' ' -f2- | tr -d '\r')"
  cdn="$(printf '%s' "$hdr" | grep -iE '^(cf-ray|x-amz-cf-id|x-cache|x-served-by|x-fastly|x-sucuri-id|x-iinfo):' | cut -d: -f1 | paste -sd, -)"
  bh="$(sha256sum /tmp/.vo_body | cut -c1-12)"
  echo "${status:-?}|${len:-0}|${server:--}|${cdn:--}|${bh}"
}

echo "[*] via CDN   (normal DNS):"
CDN_R="$(probe '')"; echo "    $CDN_R"
echo "[*] via origin (--resolve ${HOST}:${PORT}:${IP}):"
ORI_R="$(probe "--resolve ${HOST}:${PORT}:${IP}")"; echo "    $ORI_R"

IFS='|' read -r cs cl _ ccdn cbh <<<"$CDN_R"
IFS='|' read -r os ol _ ocdn obh <<<"$ORI_R"
echo
if [ "$cbh" = "$obh" ] && [ "$obh" != "$(sha256sum < /dev/null | cut -c1-12)" ]; then
  echo "[=] BODY MATCH -> $IP very likely the ORIGIN for $HOST"
elif [ "$cs" = "$os" ] && [ "$ocdn" = "-" ] && [ "$ccdn" != "-" ]; then
  echo "[=] same status, candidate has NO CDN headers -> likely origin"
else
  echo "[~] inconclusive: compare status/len/body above manually"
fi
```

Run:
```bash
./verify_origin.sh target.example.com 203.0.113.45 /
./verify_origin.sh target.example.com 203.0.113.45 /login 443
```

entries.title: Cloud bucket permutation brute (S3 / GCS / Azure)
S3 bucket anonymous access probe (list/read/write)
entries.body:
Checks the **access level** of a found S3 bucket anonymously (without AWS keys), using `aws s3 ls s3://NAME --no-sign-request`. It runs three checks: (1) **list** - object listing; (2) **read** - downloading the first object; (3) **write** - an attempt to upload a probe file (dangerous, done only with a flag). It handles the region redirect (`AuthorizationHeaderMalformed`/301): it extracts the correct region and retries the request with `--region`. This is the next step after `bucket_brute.py`: to learn what exactly is open.

> A `awscli` install is required, but **keys are not needed** thanks to `--no-sign-request`. The `[w]` write test is marked destructive: it creates an object in someone else's bucket, so do it only within an authorized scope and with the explicit `--write` flag.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bucket_access.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Probe anonymous access level of an S3 bucket (list / read / optional write).
set -u
NAME="${1:?usage: bucket_access.sh <bucket-name> [--write]}"
DO_WRITE=0; [ "${2:-}" = "--write" ] && DO_WRITE=1
command -v aws >/dev/null || { echo "[!] need awscli: pip install awscli"; exit 1; }

REGION_ARG=""
ls_try(){ aws s3 ls "s3://${NAME}" --no-sign-request $REGION_ARG 2>&1; }

echo "[*] bucket: $NAME"
OUT="$(ls_try)"
# handle region redirect
if printf '%s' "$OUT" | grep -qiE 'AuthorizationHeaderMalformed|PermanentRedirect|301'; then
  REG="$(printf '%s' "$OUT" | grep -oiE "expecting '[a-z0-9-]+'" | grep -oE "[a-z0-9-]+'" | tr -d \"'\" | head -1)"
  [ -z "$REG" ] && REG="$(printf '%s' "$OUT" | grep -oE 'us-[a-z]+-[0-9]|eu-[a-z]+-[0-9]|ap-[a-z]+-[0-9]' | head -1)"
  if [ -n "$REG" ]; then echo "    [i] region redirect -> $REG"; REGION_ARG="--region $REG"; OUT="$(ls_try)"; fi
fi

# [l] LIST
if printf '%s' "$OUT" | grep -qiE 'AccessDenied'; then
  echo "    [l] LIST   : DENIED (bucket exists, listing blocked)"
elif printf '%s' "$OUT" | grep -qiE 'NoSuchBucket'; then
  echo "    [l] LIST   : bucket does not exist"; exit 0
else
  echo "    [l] LIST   : ALLOWED"
  printf '%s\n' "$OUT" | sed 's/^/        /' | head -10
fi

# [r] READ first object
FIRST="$(printf '%s' "$OUT" | awk '/[0-9]+ /{print $NF}' | grep -v '/$' | head -1)"
if [ -n "$FIRST" ]; then
  if aws s3 cp "s3://${NAME}/${FIRST}" /tmp/.ba_obj --no-sign-request $REGION_ARG >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "    [r] READ   : ALLOWED ($FIRST -> /tmp/.ba_obj, $(wc -c </tmp/.ba_obj) bytes)"
  else
    echo "    [r] READ   : denied for $FIRST"
  fi
fi

# [w] WRITE (destructive, opt-in)
if [ "$DO_WRITE" = "1" ]; then
  TF="/tmp/.ars3nal_write_test"; echo "ars3nal-write-test" > "$TF"
  if aws s3 cp "$TF" "s3://${NAME}/ars3nal_write_test.txt" --no-sign-request $REGION_ARG >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "    [w] WRITE  : ALLOWED !! (uploaded ars3nal_write_test.txt - clean it up)"
  else
    echo "    [w] WRITE  : denied"
  fi
fi
```

Run:
```bash
pip install awscli
./bucket_access.sh acme-backups
# write test (destructive, only within scope):
./bucket_access.sh acme-backups --write
```

entries.title: S3 buckets from JS bundles (harvest)
entries.body:
Harvest **S3 buckets from JS bundles**: pulls JS (by URL or from a gau/katana file), regex-extracts bucket names from links like `bucket.s3[.-]region.amazonaws.com` (virtual-hosted) and `s3.../bucket` (path-style), dedups. Complements the anon-access probe: first find the buckets the app actually uses, then test access.

> Covers dot/dash/website/dualstack and path-style. Next: `aws s3 ls s3://<bucket> --no-sign-request` (see the sibling anon-access script), s3scanner, the grayhatwarfare/osint.sh indexes, dorks.

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{JS_URL}}`, `{{LIST}}` (file of JS URLs).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# s3_js_harvest.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, re, sys, requests

VH = re.compile(r'([a-z0-9][a-z0-9.\-]{1,61}[a-z0-9])\.s3[.\-]?(?:[a-z0-9\-]+\.)?amazonaws\.com', re.I)
PS = re.compile(r's3[.\-]?(?:[a-z0-9\-]+\.)?amazonaws\.com/([a-z0-9][a-z0-9.\-]{1,61}[a-z0-9])', re.I)

def sources(args):
    if args.list:
        for line in open(args.list, encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore"):
            u = line.strip()
            if u:
                yield u
    for u in args.url or []:
        yield u

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL S3 URL harvester from JS")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", action="append", help="JS URL (repeatable)")
    ap.add_argument("-l", "--list", help="file of JS URLs (from gau/katana)")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=15)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    if not (a.url or a.list):
        sys.exit("give -u <jsurl> or -l <listfile>")
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()
    buckets = set()
    for u in sources(a):
        try:
            t = requests.get(u, timeout=a.timeout, verify=False).text
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            print(f"  ? {u} ({e})", file=sys.stderr); continue
        for m in VH.finditer(t):
            buckets.add(m.group(1).lower())
        for m in PS.finditer(t):
            buckets.add(m.group(1).lower())
    for b in sorted(buckets):
        print(b)
    print(f"\n[=] {len(buckets)} candidate bucket(s). Next:", file=sys.stderr)
    print("    aws s3 ls s3://<bucket> --no-sign-request   (see the anon-access probe script)", file=sys.stderr)
    print("    s3scanner -bucket-file buckets.txt -enumerate   (github.com/sa7mon/S3Scanner)", file=sys.stderr)
    print("    indexes: buckets.grayhatwarfare.com , osint.sh/buckets", file=sys.stderr)
    print('    dorks: site:s3.amazonaws.com "target" | GitHub: ".s3.amazonaws.com" org:TARGET', file=sys.stderr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 s3_js_harvest.py -u https://target.com/static/js/main.js
gau target.com | grep -Ei '\.js$' > js.txt && python3 s3_js_harvest.py -l js.txt > buckets.txt
```
